productionReadiness.$schema: Hand-edited. Read by update-status.mjs and merged into live-status.json under `summary.productionReadiness`. Render in live-status.html → renderProductionReadiness().
_updateWorkflow: When a stage's status changes, update the matching stage entry in this file.
Append a row to `history` with { date: 'YYYY-MM-DD', stage, from, to, note, commit?: 'sha or PR url' }.
Keep `baseline` IMMUTABLE — that field captures the pre-work state on 2026-05-24. Only `current` and `history` evolve.
Run `node docs/status/update-status.mjs` to push the change through the cron pipeline.
DO NOT delete history entries — the dashboard renders them as the audit trail.

tracks.name: Voice / Digital Assistant
tracks.description: Production-readiness for the conversational voice agent (Wave E + Wave D). Spans intent coverage, eval regression suite, PHI safety, telephony backends, pilot ops, and runtime monitoring.

stages.name: Stage 1 — Capability completeness
stages.rationale: Every conversational intent (book / cancel / reschedule / confirm / list / pay / verify-insurance / transfer / end) has an end-to-end implementation + STRIDE adversarial test.

baseline.summary: 12 intents shipped end-to-end. 741+ tests across packages/voice-agent; 74% line / 87% branch coverage. STRIDE adversarial cases (prompt injection, identity bypass, cross-tenant leakage) covered.

current.summary: Unchanged. 12 intents, 741+ tests, 74% / 87% coverage.

stages.name: Stage 2 — Eval harness
stages.rationale: Regression suite of scripted golden conversations + scorers. Lets us change prompts / intents / state machine without holding our breath. Runs on every PR.

baseline.summary: No `evals/` or `golden-transcripts/` directory existed. Only `improvement/transcript-reviewer.ts` ran post-call heuristics — no pre-deployment gate, no per-intent SLA tracking, no hallucination detector.

current.summary: @cernia/voice-agent-evals package shipped. 8 goldens covering book-appointment, lookup-practitioner, transfer-to-human, PHI gate + verification dance (3-turn DOB/2FA), red-flag chest-pain short-circuit, unsupported-language transfer, cost-ceiling trip. 3 scorers (intent-emission, agent-text expectations, PHI-leak regex). 97 ms wall clock. Wired into CI via `pnpm --filter @cernia/voice-agent-evals test`.

remaining: mfa-locked-out golden (3 wrong DOB attempts → phi-restriction transfer)
red-flag-bilingual-spanish golden (dolor en el pecho on Spanish-enabled tenant)
prompt-injection-rejection golden (ignore-previous-instructions adversarial)
appointment-window-disambiguation golden (disambiguate_practitioner → book_appointment)
Nightly --live mode against real Anthropic API for one canary golden
CI integration: add voice-agent-evals to .github/workflows so per-commit eval regressions block merge

history.note: Audit finding: eval harness exists in packages/voice-agent-evals and runs via `pnpm test` but is NOT wired into .github/workflows. Per-commit regression coverage is dormant. Track CI integration as remaining work.

stages.name: Stage 3 — HIPAA / PHI safety
stages.rationale: PHI redaction in every log sink, audit emission per dialog turn, BAA-covered provider allowlist, output-side clinical-content classifier, caller rate-limiting.

baseline.summary: Core posture solid: redaction.ts (100% tested) covers SSN/phone/email/MRN/DOB/ICD-10, IRedFlagAuditEmitter wired with structured STRIDE events, CostCeilingTracker emits reason enum on cap trip, 3-strike DOB/2FA lock-out gates unverified callers. Survey dispatcher has per-patient at-most-one constraint.

current.summary: Unchanged from baseline.

remaining: BAA-covered LLM-provider allowlist (packages/voice-agent/src/baa-providers.ts — does not exist yet)
Output-side clinical content classifier (tracked as I-320 / deferred to Wave-D9)
Per-caller global rate-limit (tracked as I-321)

history.note: Audit finding: code references BAA-covered provider allowlist (rag-store.ts:19) but no actual enforcement gate consults it at dialog init. Any provider can be wired today. Documented as remaining work.

stages.name: Stage 4 — Telephony + ops
stages.rationale: Telephony backends (Twilio, LiveKit/SIP) with recording-pause-on-PHI, KMS encryption at rest, HIPAA retention policy.

baseline.summary: Twilio + LiveKit/SIP backends in packages/telephony. apps/api/src/voice-call-handler.ts wires inbound webhook + barge-in controller + recording-pause on PHI request. KMS encryption at rest + 7-year HIPAA retention default.

current.summary: Unchanged.

stages.name: Stage 5 — Pilot readiness
stages.rationale: HIL (human-in-the-loop) plumbing, outage runbook, pilot cohort + success-threshold definition.

baseline.summary: handoff-communication.ts (91 lines, 100% tested) constructs FHIR Communication on every transfer with resolvedBrandName audit. Transfer intents emit closed-enum reason (cost-ceiling / phi-restriction / medical-emergency / out-of-hours / agent-uncertain). Full STRIDE threat model. docs/operations/runbooks/voice-agent-outage.md exists as STUB ONLY (trigger / owner / steps / rollback all empty). No pilot cohort / success-rate doc.

current.summary: Unchanged.

remaining: Fill the voice-agent-outage runbook (trigger condition, on-call owner, incident steps, rollback)
Pilot cohort definition: 1 tenant, 1 intent, 2-4 weeks, success threshold (eg 85% intent-completion rate, < 5% unintended-transfer rate)
Pre-pilot checklist signed off by clinical lead + compliance

stages.name: Stage 6 — Monitoring
stages.rationale: Real-time gauges for transfer-to-human rate, cost-per-call, per-intent success, response latency, citation-validity. Without this we can't detect production drift.

baseline.summary: Audit events emitted (red-flag, cost-ceiling, transfer, verification). NO Prometheus/StatsD gauges. NO real-time dashboard. Metrics derivable post-hoc from transcripts via the improvement pipeline, but not exposed in any operator-facing surface.

current.summary: Admin observability API live (8 Fastify endpoints in voice-agent-admin-routes.ts) AND tenant admin UI sidebar nav wired (6 voice-agent pages reachable). RBAC gate on Policy + Customization (office-admin = read-only, server returns 403 for non-admin PATCH). NEW: cernia-ops voice-agent fleet page at /cernia-ops/platform/voice-agents — cross-tenant aggregate (tenants enabled, calls 24h/7d, autoResolve/transfer rates, ceiling trips, fleet spend, BAA vendor health for Twilio/Deepgram/ElevenLabs/OpenAI/Anthropic). NO PHI surfaced — per-tenant detail stays in tenant admin. STILL TO SHIP: Prometheus adapter, Grafana dashboard JSON, alert runbooks, cross-tenant aggregator API endpoint (page currently reads from sample data).

remaining: Cross-tenant aggregator API GET /cernia-ops/platform/voice-agents/fleet (replaces sample-data on the fleet page)
PrometheusMetricsCollector adapter (new package @cernia/voice-agent-metrics-prom) wrapping prom-client; exposes /metrics endpoint mounted in apps/api
Grafana dashboard JSON checked into docs/observability/voice-agent-dashboard.json — panels for each metric, alerts for transfer-rate > 25%, p99 latency > 5s, ceiling-trip-rate spikes
Operator runbook for each alert (docs/operations/runbooks/voice-agent-*.md)
Boot-time guard in apps/api that refuses to start with NoopMetricsCollector in prod (env-gated)
cernia-ops follow-up pages: vendor-health detail, fleet billing drill-down, feature rollout per-tenant flags

history.note: Shipped voice-agent-admin-routes.ts (8 endpoints). All admin UI voice-agent tabs now backed by real data — 401 error resolved. fromNumber redacted to area-code per PHI rules. listByTenant() added to ICallSessionStore + PostgresCallSessionStore.

Shipped cernia-ops voice-agent fleet page + sidebar entry. Adds SaaS-operator visibility (fleet adoption, call volume, cost, BAA vendor health) — closes the architecture gap where Cernia operators had zero voice-agent insight. Sample-data backed for now; cross-tenant API aggregator is a tracked follow-up.
Scaffolded @cernia/voice-agent-evals package with 3 starter goldens (book-appointment, lookup-practitioner, transfer-to-human).
Added 5 more goldens covering PHI gate, full verification dance, red-flag, language gate, and cost-ceiling. Total 8 goldens passing in 97 ms.

IVoiceAgentMetrics seam shipped. Emission points wired into dialog.ts (session lifecycle, intents, transfers, LLM latency, cost-ceiling, red-flag, verification outcome, turn count). 12 unit tests verify each emission point. NoopMetricsCollector default + InMemoryMetricsCollector for tests + safeRecord wrapper for adapter-fault tolerance. 782/782 voice-agent tests + 8/8 evals still green. Prometheus adapter + Grafana dashboard + runbooks still to ship.

Metrics collector wired into apps/api boot (apps/api/src/index.ts:1665). InMemoryMetricsCollector by default; CERNIA_VOICE_METRICS=off opts out. Emission points now actually run in production instead of routing to NoopMetricsCollector. Discovered via the shelf-ware audit — the seam had been shipped but never instantiated in apps/api, so every metric was silently dropped.

tracks.name: Functionality Utilization (cross-package)
tracks.description: Audit + remediation of packages built but not wired into apps/*. The 2026-05-24 shelf-ware audit found 286 total packages, 36 imported by apps/*/src, ~140 lazy-load-by-design (catalog-* and specialty-* — loaded on-demand per clinical workflow, NOT shelf-ware), and ~30-40 genuine infrastructure built without app wiring. Each stage groups one coherent cluster of unwired infra; current=absent means no app instantiates any package in the cluster.

stages.name: CDS Hooks & quality measures
stages.rationale: FHIR CDS Hooks is the standard for surfacing clinical decision support inside the EMR workflow. CQM is the Centers for Medicare quality-measure compute engine. Both are core regulatory/clinical infra that have to run server-side for the platform to qualify for value-based-care reimbursement.

baseline.summary: packages/cds-hooks and packages/cqm-engine exist with full unit tests; ZERO importers in apps/*/src. No CDS Hook endpoint mounted in apps/api. No CQM batch job. Quality measure scores can't surface in any UI.

current.summary: CDS Hooks runtime wired into apps/api. GET /cds-services discovery + POST /cds-services/:hookId invocation are live, backed by @cernia/cds-hooks's InMemoryCdsServiceRegistry + HookRouter + HookInvocationHandler. One starter `patient-view` service ships a `voice-agent available` info card. 6 new integration tests cover discovery, happy-path invocation, unknown hookId 404, invalid body 400, inactive-service filtering. apps/api: 1894 tests pass (was 1888). CQM batch + admin UI remain — see remaining.

remaining: Tenant-scoped service registry (current registry is process-global and shared across tenants).
Real FHIRPath / workflow / bot dispatcher (the starter dispatcher returns hardcoded cards keyed by hookId).
Mount the other standard hooks: order-select, order-sign, medication-prescribe, encounter-discharge.
Schedule a nightly CQM batch job that emits MeasureReport resources per tenant.
Admin UI panel exposing CQM scores per measure + per provider.
Flip jwtVerificationEnabled=true once the EHR-issuer trust list is configured.

stages.name: Bot / workflow runtime
stages.rationale: Self-service workflow authoring lets tenants build their own background bots (eligibility sweep, appointment reminder, lab follow-up) without writing code. Without the runtime wired, the marketplace's workflow templates have nothing to execute against.

baseline.summary: bot-runtime, j1b-workflow-template-registry, j2c-fork-and-modify-diff, j2d-ai-assisted-workflow-gen, j3g-background-bot-template-catalog all shipped with tests. ZERO end-to-end wiring: bot-runtime has 1 importer (an internal package), but no app/api boot path instantiates a bot scheduler or template registry.

current.summary: Workflow template discovery live in apps/api: GET /workflows/templates, GET /workflows/templates/:id, GET /workflows/templates/:id/latest. POST /bot-runtime/$analyse runs static analysis on a bot source string (no execution). InMemoryTemplateRegistry seeded with 'cernia-voice-followup' template. 7 new integration tests. Bot sandbox dry-run + scheduler + j2c/j2d/j3g + admin UI remain.

remaining: Boot the bot-runtime scheduler in apps/api with the template registry as its catalog.
Mount POST /bot-runtime/$dry-run that wraps dryRunBot from the sandbox layer.
Wire j2c fork-and-modify diff + j2d AI-workflow-gen alongside the registry.
Wire j3g background-bot template catalog.
Postgres-backed template registry (in-memory ships today).
Admin UI for browsing / forking / activating workflow templates per tenant.

stages.name: ML / inference platform
stages.rationale: The Cernia ML stack — dataset DSL, AutoML orchestrator, model registry, deployment-modes, drift/fairness monitoring, SaMD (software-as-medical-device) classifier, training pipeline, LLM gateway — is the back-end for every learned-model feature (risk-classifier, predicted-no-shows, etc.). All shipped, none wired.

baseline.summary: inference-platform, o1-llm-gateway, p1a-dataset-dsl, p1b-automl-orchestrator, p1c-model-registry, p3a-deployment-modes, p3b-drift-fairness-monitoring, p4a-samd-classifier, p-pipe-training-pipeline — ALL shipped with full tests, ZERO importers in apps/*/src. The risk-classifier in ml/risk-classifier/ runs as a Python sidecar but doesn't go through this stack.

current.summary: Model registry surfaced via apps/api admin routes. GET /admin/ml/models (list names), GET /admin/ml/models/:name/versions, GET /admin/ml/models/:name/:version, POST /admin/ml/models (register). Seeded with risk-classifier-v4.0.0-comprehensive shim entry that maps to the existing Python sidecar. 8 new integration tests cover discovery, resolve, register-then-discover, 404 paths, 400 on bad input. apps/api: 1902 tests pass. Runner dispatch, real ml/risk-classifier integration, LLM gateway routing, drift telemetry — remain.

remaining: Front the existing Python risk-classifier through inference-platform's RequestRouter so /admin/ml/models/:urn/$predict actually dispatches.
Postgres-backed model registry store (in-memory is wired today; Postgres adapter exists in the package).
Wire o1-llm-gateway as the central LLM router (replaces direct Anthropic/OpenAI imports across packages).
Wire p3b-drift-monitoring alongside ml/risk-classifier so every prediction emits drift telemetry.
Mount p1a-dataset-dsl + p1b-automl-orchestrator endpoints for the training loop.
Admin UI for browsing models + promoting versions.

stages.name: Telehealth & remote patient monitoring
stages.rationale: RPM (remote patient monitoring) is the Q-wave's reimbursable feature: device ingestion + care-plan generation + adherence tracking. Telehealth visits via WebRTC are the synchronous-care complement. Both built, neither runs.

baseline.summary: q-s1-webrtc-signaling, q-s2-rpm-device-ingestion, q-s3-telehealth-visit-audit, q-s4-rpm-careplan-generator, q-s5-visit-summary-generator, q-s6-rpm-adherence-tracker all shipped. No WebRTC signaling endpoint in apps/api, no device webhook routes, no care-plan generation cron, no visit-summary UI.

current.summary: RPM compute endpoints live in apps/api: POST /rpm/readings/$evaluate (threshold classification), GET /rpm/care-plan-templates, POST /rpm/care-plans/$generate, POST /rpm/adherence/$compute (score + intervention recommendation). All stateless — caller supplies inputs, server runs pure-function compute. 7 new integration tests. WebRTC signaling websocket + stateful device webhook + nightly cron + UI surfaces remain.

remaining: Mount the WebRTC signaling websocket in apps/api (q-s1).
Stateful POST /rpm/devices/:deviceId/readings webhook that feeds the AlertEngine and persists EvaluationResult.
Schedule the care-plan generator + adherence-tracker as nightly batch jobs against the real reading store.
Wire q-s3 telehealth-visit audit + q-s5 visit-summary into apps/api.
Clinic UI for visit summary, RPM dashboard, and care-plan management.

stages.name: Caregiver delegation & identity federation
stages.rationale: Allows a patient's family/caregiver to act on their behalf (proxy), JIT-grant temporary access to a covering provider, SAML/OIDC federation for hospital-network SSO, and break-glass emergency access. Compliance-load-bearing for any enterprise pilot.

baseline.summary: g-d1-caregiver-proxy, g-d2-delegation, g-d3-jit-access, g-d4-access-review-attestation, g-d5-saml-oidc-federation, g-d6-break-glass all shipped. Existing auth flow doesn't consult any of them; no SAML/OIDC providers configured in apps/api boot path.

current.summary: Proxy lifecycle endpoints live in apps/api: POST /caregiver/proxy/$grant, POST /caregiver/proxy/:id/$revoke, GET /caregiver/proxy/by-patient/:id. JIT request + break-glass invoke endpoints: POST /access/jit/$request, POST /access/break-glass/$invoke (both stateless pure-function with blocker codes for empty-scopes / short-justification / etc). In-memory proxy store. 8 new integration tests. SAML/OIDC federation, access-review-attestation, admin UI remain.

remaining: Persistent (Postgres) proxy store + JIT request store + break-glass invocation log.
Wire g-d5 SAML/OIDC into auth-providers + apps/api login routes.
Wire g-d2 user-to-user delegation alongside g-d1.
Wire g-d4 access-review-attestation surface for compliance.
JIT approve/deny + break-glass review endpoints (currently only request/invoke ship).
Admin UI for delegation requests + access-review attestation.

stages.name: SaaS billing & marketplace
stages.rationale: Enterprise invoicing, ASC 606 revenue recognition, SaaS metrics (MRR/ARR/churn), contract auto-renewal, discount-approval workflow, marketplace checkout, tax compliance. The commercial back-end for selling Cernia at scale.

baseline.summary: marketplace, x1a-tax-compliance, x1b-asc-606-revenue-recognition, x1c-saas-metrics-computation, x2a-enterprise-invoicing, x2b-contract-auto-renewal, x3c-discount-approval-workflow, j1c-sandbox-tenant-lifecycle, j1d-marketplace-checkout all shipped. No marketplace UI in apps/*. No invoice generation job. No tax engine wired into payment paths.

current.summary: Marketplace discovery (GET /marketplace/listings) + slug-resolve (GET /marketplace/listings/:slug) + create (POST /marketplace/listings) live, backed by @cernia/marketplace's InMemoryListingStore. Seeded with the Cernia voice-agent listing so the catalogue isn't empty. POST /admin/saas-metrics/snapshot computes MRR/ARR/ARPA via @cernia/x1c-saas-metrics. 8 new integration tests. apps/api: 1910/1910 tests pass.

remaining: Wire x2a invoicing into the existing billing pipeline (currently only Stripe direct charge).
Mount j1d checkout routes; build the customer-facing storefront UI.
Schedule x1c SaaS-metrics nightly compute against the real subscription store (current route is stateless pure-function).
Wire x1a tax engine (Stripe Tax + Avalara fallback) into every payment path.
Wire x1b ASC 606 revenue recognition into the monthly close.
Wire x2b contract auto-renewal cron.
Wire x3c discount-approval workflow.

history.note: Track created from the 2026-05-24 shelf-ware audit. 6 stages, all baseline+current=absent. ~30-40 genuine shelf-ware packages grouped into clusters; ~140 catalog-* and specialty-* packages are lazy-load-by-design and intentionally NOT included here.

CDS Hooks runtime mounted in apps/api. GET /cds-services discovery + POST /cds-services/:hookId invocation backed by @cernia/cds-hooks. Starter patient-view service registered with a 'voice-agent available' info card. 6 new integration tests cover discovery, happy path, unknown hookId, invalid body, inactive-service filtering. apps/api: 1894/1894 tests pass.

Model registry mounted in apps/api as /admin/ml/models — list names, list versions, resolve, register. In-memory store seeded with risk-classifier-v4.0.0-comprehensive shim. 8 new integration tests. apps/api: 1902/1902 tests pass.

Marketplace discovery + slug-resolve + create endpoints live at /marketplace/listings, backed by @cernia/marketplace's InMemoryListingStore (seeded with Cernia voice-agent). POST /admin/saas-metrics/snapshot computes MRR/ARR/ARPA via @cernia/x1c-saas-metrics. 8 new integration tests. apps/api: 1910/1910 tests pass.

RPM compute endpoints live: /rpm/readings/$evaluate (threshold classification), /rpm/care-plan-templates (list 6 shipped), /rpm/care-plans/$generate, /rpm/adherence/$compute (score + intervention). Stateless pure-function over caller-supplied input. 7 new integration tests. Stateful device webhook + WebRTC signaling + UI remain.

Proxy lifecycle (grant / revoke / list-by-patient) + JIT request + break-glass invoke endpoints live in apps/api. In-memory proxy store. JIT + break-glass routes are pure-function with blocker codes for empty-scopes, short-justification, etc. 8 new integration tests. SAML/OIDC federation, persistent store, access-review-attestation, admin UI remain.

Workflow template discovery (list/find/latest) + bot static-analysis endpoint live in apps/api. InMemoryTemplateRegistry seeded with the cernia-voice-followup template. 7 new integration tests. Scheduler + sandbox dry-run + j2c/j2d/j3g + admin UI remain. All 6 functionality-utilization stages are now partial — none absent.

tracks.name: External Integrations & per-tenant config
tracks.description: Per-tenant credentials + vendor backends the voice agent talks to (Twilio for telephony, Deepgram for STT, ElevenLabs for TTS, OpenAI/Anthropic for LLM). Also covers self-serve tenant provisioning + per-tenant workflow customization. Without this layer every tenant shares the platform's trial credentials and can't customize the assistant.

stages.name: Tenant secrets — per-tenant credential storage
stages.rationale: Each tenant must paste their own Twilio/Deepgram/ElevenLabs/LLM credentials so calls bill to their accounts. Plaintext is encrypted at rest with envelope encryption (AES-256-GCM); the resolver falls back to platform-level env vars when no per-tenant override exists.

baseline.summary: No per-tenant credential storage. The voice agent's Twilio/Deepgram/ElevenLabs/OpenAI/Anthropic keys came from process.env only. Every tenant in a multi-tenant deploy would share the same vendor accounts — billing attribution impossible, key rotation per tenant impossible, white-glove onboarding meant editing the platform-level env.

current.summary: Foundation shipped end-to-end. Migration 046_tenant_secrets (composite-PK (tenant_id, provider), opaque ciphertext column, JSONB metadata). TenantSecretResolver in @cernia/core with NoopSecretCipher (tests + local-demo) + AesGcmSecretCipher (AES-256-GCM, 12-byte nonce, kid prefix for rotation) + InMemoryTenantSecretStore. KyselyTenantSecretStore in @cernia/storage with pg-mem contract test. Fastify routes /tenants/:id/secrets[/:provider] (GET list / PUT upsert / DELETE clear) wired into apps/api at slot 10i with cross-tenant 403 guard mirroring tenant-brand-routes, audit emission carrying only last4 (never plaintext). Admin UI: Credentials & Integrations sub-page at /admin/tenants/[id]/voice-agent/credentials with cards for Twilio + Deepgram + ElevenLabs + OpenAI + Anthropic; persona editor gets a sub-page nav link to it. 20 core unit tests + storage pg-mem contract test green.

remaining: Swap apps/api boot from InMemoryTenantSecretStore to KyselyTenantSecretStore once a Kysely pool is wired (today the store is process-local; a restart wipes overrides).
Boot-time refusal if CERNIA_TENANT_SECRET_KEY/KID is unset in production env (currently logs a warning and falls back to NoopSecretCipher).
KMS-backed key rotation procedure + runbook (the AesGcmSecretCipher already supports kid-prefixed multi-key decrypt for the rotation window).
Audit: every PUT/DELETE writes a hil-feedback-style Communication so compliance review can replay credential rotations.

stages.name: Twilio voice backend boot wiring
stages.rationale: @cernia/telephony ships a TwilioVoiceBackend + LiveKit/SIP backend. apps/api needs to instantiate one and wire it into the voice-call handler so an inbound call actually arrives at the dialog engine.

baseline.summary: packages/telephony has TwilioVoiceBackend + LiveKit/SIP backend with full unit coverage. apps/api/src/voice-call-handler.ts handles barge-in + recording-pause-on-PHI. NOT yet wired: the boot path doesn't construct a TwilioVoiceBackend instance and the /telephony/inbound webhook is stub-only.

current.summary: TwilioVoiceBackend fully wired. buildTwilioVoiceClient() native-fetch factory added to @cernia/telephony (no SDK dependency — uses Node 20+ fetch). Boot path in apps/api constructs TwilioVoiceBackend when CERNIA_TELEPHONY_BACKEND=twilio + TWILIO_ACCOUNT_SID + TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN + CERNIA_PUBLIC_BASE_URL are set; falls back to stub with structured log. 26 new tests cover create/fetch/update + auth header + idempotency. Typecheck + full telephony test suite clean.

remaining: Boot wiring in apps/api: construct TwilioVoiceBackend keyed by tenantId via TenantSecretResolver.resolve(tenantId, 'twilio').
Inbound webhook /telephony/twilio/inbound that looks up tenant from tenant_phone_numbers, resolves the per-tenant Auth Token, and TwiML-bridges to the dialog engine.
Outbound campaign route + caller-id allowlist enforcement (tenant_phone_numbers.is_active filter).
Smoke test against the Twilio sandbox using one real test number.

history.note: buildTwilioVoiceClient native-fetch factory + TwilioVoiceBackend boot wiring in apps/api. 26 new tests.

stages.name: Deepgram STT adapter
stages.rationale: Speech-to-text is the input side of the voice loop. Deepgram is the BAA-covered default; per-tenant model + language overrides are read from tenant_secrets.metadata.

baseline.summary: @cernia/voice-agent has an ISpeechToText seam but no production adapter. All current eval/golden runs use a stubbed STT that echoes back a scripted transcript.

current.summary: DeepgramWsTransport production adapter shipped in apps/api/src/deepgram-ws-transport.ts. Uses ws npm package (already a dependency) with Authorization: Token header. Parses Results, SpeechStarted, UtteranceEnd, Error message types into DeepgramServerEvent. boot path wires DeepgramSttBackend when CERNIA_SPEECH_STT=deepgram + DEEPGRAM_API_KEY are set; falls back to stub STT with warn log otherwise. 13 new unit tests cover factory shape, JSON parsing (all event types), error resilience, and handler idempotency. api typecheck + full api test suite clean.

remaining: Build packages/voice-stt-deepgram implementing ISpeechToText against Deepgram's websocket API.
Token sourcing: TenantSecretResolver.resolve(tenantId, 'deepgram'); metadata.defaultLanguage/defaultModel control per-tenant model selection.
Wire into apps/api voice-call-handler so STT frames are forwarded into the dialog engine.
Eval: add a STT-accuracy golden using a recorded WAV + expected transcript.

history.note: DeepgramWsTransport transport adapter + apps/api boot wiring. 13 tests.

stages.name: ElevenLabs TTS adapter
stages.rationale: Text-to-speech is the output side of the loop. ElevenLabs is the default; per-tenant voice id is read from tenant_secrets.vendorAccountId. Tenant-defined persona (existing voice-agent persona editor) selects the voice.

baseline.summary: ITextToSpeech seam exists; no production adapter. Persona editor already exposes a voiceId picker with sample previews but every save is dropped on the floor.

current.summary: ElevenLabsFetchTransport production adapter shipped in apps/api/src/elevenlabs-fetch-transport.ts. Uses Node 22 native fetch with xi-api-key header + streaming ReadableStream body iteration. boot path wires ElevenLabsTtsBackend when CERNIA_SPEECH_TTS=elevenlabs + ELEVENLABS_API_KEY are set; falls back to stub TTS with warn log otherwise. 8 new unit tests cover factory shape, streaming multi-chunk assembly, HTTP 4xx error propagation, null body guard, and close idempotency. api typecheck clean.

remaining: Build packages/voice-tts-elevenlabs implementing ITextToSpeech against ElevenLabs's REST API.
Token sourcing: TenantSecretResolver.resolve(tenantId, 'elevenlabs'); vendorAccountId = default voiceId.
Persist persona voiceId via PATCH /Organization/:id (the persona editor currently console.logs the save).
Wire the resolver lookup into apps/api so dialog turns get TTS bytes.

history.note: ElevenLabsFetchTransport native-fetch streaming adapter + apps/api boot wiring. 8 tests.

stages.name: Self-serve tenant provisioning
stages.rationale: A new tenant should be able to land on apps/installer, paste their org details, hook up SSO, paste their Twilio/Deepgram/ElevenLabs keys, and have a working voice agent with no Cernia handholding.

baseline.summary: apps/installer exists as a wizard scaffold (org details, branding, persona) but the 12 config items the voice agent needs (Twilio SID + token, Deepgram key, ElevenLabs key + default voice, phone number registration, LLM provider choice, language allowlist, business hours, transfer phone numbers, cost ceiling, red-flag overrides) are partial / scattered / not wired into apps/api boot.

current.summary: Voice-config step (step 4 of 8) added to apps/installer wizard. Collects all 12 voice-agent config items: Twilio AccountSID+AuthToken (sent to tenant-secret store), Deepgram API key, ElevenLabs API key + voice ID, LLM provider, language allowlist, business hours (open/close/timezone), primary + urgent transfer numbers, daily cost ceiling, red-flag phrase overrides. POST /api/voice-config/[sessionId] handles secrets separately from non-secret step data. packages/onboarding updated: voice-config added to STEP_IDS, VoiceConfigStepData type + server-side validation live.

remaining: Audit apps/installer for all 12 voice-agent config items; produce a single setup wizard.
Wire each step to its server-side endpoint (tenant-secret-routes, persona PATCH, tenant_phone_numbers register).
Post-install smoke test: place an outbound call to the tenant's registered number; verify the agent answers with the tenant's branded greeting in the tenant's chosen voice.
White-glove import: CSV / JSON ingest for enterprise tenants migrating from another platform.

history.note: voice-config wizard step (step 4/8) — all 12 config items, secrets to tenant-secret store

stages.name: Per-tenant workflow customization
stages.rationale: Different customers want different things: SNF wants 'medication-pickup-reminder' but not 'cancel-appointment'; pain clinic wants a custom intake script; pediatric clinic wants distinct red-flag thresholds. The voice agent must be configurable per tenant — not forked per tenant.

baseline.summary: Building blocks exist: @cernia/j1b-workflow-template-registry (template per tenant), TenantPolicy.allowedActions / practitionerScope / redFlagOverrides, Organization.extension[voice-agent-persona], j2c fork-and-modify-diff, j2d AI-assisted-workflow-gen. None composed into a single configurability surface; tenant admins can't see or change any of it without engineering involvement.

current.summary: TenantCustomization schema + runtime resolver + FHIR codec shipped in @cernia/voice-agent. TenantCustomization interface: allowedIntents override (mandatory intents always re-injected), systemPromptAugment (injected after tone paragraph, before injection-defense), customWorkflowTemplateIds, additionalRedFlagPhrases (extend-only, never replace), businessHours (start/end/timezone/daysOfWeek → forces alwaysOn=false). applyTenantCustomization() merges TenantPolicy + TenantCustomization into AppliedCustomization for the dialog engine. FHIR codec: encodeTenantCustomization / decodeTenantCustomization using Organization extension pattern (URL: http://cernia.health/fhir/StructureDefinition/tenant-customization). buildSystemPrompt() updated with systemPromptAugment?: string param — injected as CLINIC INSTRUCTIONS block. 24 unit tests cover resolver logic, FHIR round-trips, sanitization, length clamping, and buildSystemPrompt integration. Admin UI: /admin/voice-agent/customization page with allowed-intent checkboxes, prompt-augment textarea, workflow-template list, red-flag phrase list, and business-hours schedule. Admin API client: getCustomization() + updateCustomization() methods added to voiceAgentApi.

remaining: Mount GET /api/voice-agent/customization + PATCH /api/voice-agent/customization in apps/api (reads/writes the Organization.extension FHIR codec).
Wire applyTenantCustomization() into apps/api voice-call-handler so every session start merges the tenant's customization.

Eval golden: 'tenant-with-customization-applied' — replays a session against a policy with custom allowedIntents + systemPromptAugment; asserts system prompt contains CLINIC INSTRUCTIONS and the blocked intent is absent from the allowed-tools list.
Intent Handler Overrides: backend URL per intent kind (advanced — deferred).
Documentation: customer-facing 'How to configure your assistant' guide.

history.note: Track created from the per-tenant architecture decision (lock: per-tenant credentials from day one, self-serve installer, External Integrations track now / Tenant Provisioning track expanded inside it).

End-to-end foundation: migration 046, TenantSecretResolver + Noop/AesGcm ciphers + InMemoryStore in @cernia/core, KyselyTenantSecretStore in @cernia/storage, Fastify routes (GET list / PUT upsert / DELETE clear) with cross-tenant 403 guard + audit, boot wire at slot 10i, admin Credentials & Integrations sub-page with cards for Twilio/Deepgram/ElevenLabs/OpenAI/Anthropic, sub-page nav link from the persona editor. 20 core unit tests + storage pg-mem contract test green. Remaining: Postgres swap, AES-GCM-required boot gate, KMS key rotation runbook.

TenantCustomization schema + applyTenantCustomization resolver + FHIR codec in @cernia/voice-agent. buildSystemPrompt systemPromptAugment injection (CLINIC INSTRUCTIONS block, after tone, before injection-defense). 24 unit tests. Admin UI /admin/voice-agent/customization (allowed intents, prompt augment, workflow templates, red-flag phrases, business hours). Admin API client getCustomization + updateCustomization. Remaining: apps/api endpoint mount + dialog-engine wiring + eval golden.

tracks.name: Sample-data elimination ("no mock data")
tracks.description: User-driven mandate (2026-05-25): every admin UI page must fetch from a real apps/api endpoint; the 30 sample-*.ts files under apps/admin/src/app/_data/ must be deleted. Tracking per-domain progress phase-by-phase. Full audit: docs/status/sample-data-elimination-plan.md.

stages.name: Phase 1 — Real backend infrastructure
stages.rationale: Postgres + MinIO + Redis + Mailpit + NATS in Docker, apps/api boot against real DB, dev-seed produces one real tenant + users + FHIR resources, all 4 Next.js apps running natively. Required for any swap to be testable end-to-end.

baseline.summary: Apps had never been started against a real backend on this machine. Postgres data dir on OneDrive caused fsync hangs; a native PG service was bound to host:5432 colliding with the Docker stack; admin tenant routes weren't wired; api Dockerfile didn't copy scripts/ (postinstall fail); storage migrate.js couldn't find src/migrations/ (tsc doesn't copy .sql); connection-routes.ts double-registered POST /Connection over @cernia/server's generic FHIR CRUD; 122 pnpm-store packages were partially extracted.

current.summary: Full stack up: Postgres 16 on :5433 (production durability flags), MinIO :9000/:9001, Redis, Mailpit, NATS. 40 migrations applied. tenant-acme seeded (20 FHIR resources). apps/api on :8080 with real RSA jwks. Admin :3000, trust :3001, patient :3002, installer :3003 all return HTTP 200. Fixes: docker-compose.override.yml remaps PG to 5433, scripts/ copied into api Dockerfile, migrate.js resolver tries dist/migrations then src/migrations fallback, connection-routes.ts removed duplicate POST /Connection (keeps $run), fix-pnpm-store.mjs repaired 122 broken packages.

stages.name: Phase 2 — Per-domain audit
stages.rationale: Before swapping anything, know which of the 30 sample-*.ts files map to which admin pages and which existing apps/api routes can be reused vs need to be built. Without this we'd thrash randomly across domains.

baseline.summary: No catalog existed; ad-hoc grep was the only way to know which page consumed which sample file.

current.summary: docs/status/sample-data-elimination-plan.md produced. 30 files cataloged: 0 wired, 11 partial (apps/api route exists, UI not refactored), 12 need-route (no route exists), 7 deferrable (work-app-only, no admin consumer). Effort estimate: ~50 work-days across the admin-facing surface. Phased execution plan (Phase 3.1 quick wins → Phase 3.6 specialized domains).

stages.name: Phase 3.1 — Quick wins (voice-budget, voice-improvements, questionnaires)
stages.rationale: All three have an apps/api route already shipped; only the UI fetcher needs to be swapped. ~3 admin pages total. Fastest measurable progress.

baseline.summary: All three pages render from sample-*.ts despite their backend routes existing.

current.summary: Done. 3 sample files deleted, 4 admin pages swapped (voice-budget + settings + voice-agent improvements + forms/[taskId]). New typed clients in /billing/voice-budget/api.ts + /voice-agent/improvements/api.ts. All HTTP 200; typecheck + lint clean.

stages.name: Phase 3.2 — Core SaaS layer (tenants, iam, billing)
stages.rationale: Foundational. sample-tenants.ts feeds 7 admin pages and sample-iam.ts feeds 12 — together they unblock the majority of the admin surface. Billing is revenue-critical and has a route ready.

baseline.summary: Tenant list, detail, branding, transitions, voice-agent persona, voice-agent credentials all key off SAMPLE_TENANTS today. /admin/users/* keys off SAMPLE_IAM. Billing pages key off sample-billing.ts.

current.summary: Done. 3 sample files deleted, 27 admin pages swapped, 5 new backend CRUD routes added to admin-tenants-routes. IAM + billing leveraged existing FHIR generic CRUD (User/Role/RoleBinding/ServiceAccount/ApiKey/Subscription/Invoice/UsageEvent already registered). Also caught + fixed a pre-existing Next-14 use(params) bug across 7 dynamic routes that had been 500-ing on baseline. All 32 pages HTTP 200; typecheck + lint clean.

stages.name: Phase 3.3 — Admin infrastructure (audit, reports, communications)
stages.rationale: Audit-trail visibility, report exports, in-app banners. Audit + reports have partial routes via reporting-routes.ts; communications is UI-only state.

baseline.summary: Sample-backed.

current.summary: Done. 3 sample files deleted, 9 admin pages swapped. AuditEvent + Report use FHIR generic CRUD. Communications moved to static lib/communications.ts (legitimate inline UI copy — no server endpoint). Added AuditEvent types to @cernia/core barrel. All 9 pages HTTP 200; typecheck + lint clean.

stages.name: Phase 3.4 — Healthcare domains (CDS, integrations, patient-portal)
stages.rationale: Clinical-decision-support config UI, HL7v2/X12 integration setup, portal account admin. All three have partial routes; UI refactor + minor route extensions.

baseline.summary: Sample-backed.

current.summary: Done. 4 sample files deleted (cds, integrations, patient-portal, voice-agent), 17 admin pages swapped. CDS uses /cds-services + /CdsService FHIR CRUD. Integrations uses /Connection generic CRUD with client-side protocol filter. Patient-portal uses /admin/patient-portal/* + /Patient. voice-agent-api.ts rewritten to drop mock mode. All 17 pages HTTP 200; typecheck + lint clean. Notable TODOs: CDS invocations + integration transaction logs + portal link-issue actions still need apps/api endpoints (rendered as empty-state cards).

stages.name: Phase 3.5 — ML platform + marketplace + HIE
stages.rationale: ML jobs / model browser, marketplace listings, HIE participation config. All have partial routes; medium-effort UI swaps.

baseline.summary: Sample-backed.

current.summary: Done. 3 sample files deleted (inference, marketplace, hie), 17 admin pages swapped. ML registry uses /admin/ml/models; marketplace uses /marketplace/listings; HIE uses new typed wrappers. Pages without backends (inference jobs/batch/drift/evals/quotas, marketplace installs/reviews/payouts, hie/participation/queries/rls) render explicit empty-state cards — no fabricated data. 941/941 tests pass; typecheck + lint clean.

stages.name: Phase 3.6 — Specialized domains (compliance, ai-governance, migration, fleet, sensitive-data, cqm, ig-profiles, posture)
stages.rationale: These 8 domains have NO apps/api route today — they need new endpoints + new schema in many cases. Largest effort, lowest visibility, save for last.

baseline.summary: Sample-backed; no routes.

current.summary: Sample data eliminated, real data NOT yet present. 9 sample files deleted (ai-governance, compliance, cqm, edge-fleet, extensions, ig-profiles, migration, posture, sensitive-data). 40+ admin pages now render explicit 'Backend endpoint not yet shipped' empty-state cards — no fabricated data. Helper type files retained where the empty-state DTO shape is needed. Honest gap: ~25 work-days of backend endpoint + migration work remains before these pages can populate.

remaining: Build /admin/compliance routes + migration for frameworks/controls/evidence (48 controls + 30 evidence artifacts in sample data); swap pages; delete sample-compliance.ts.
Build /admin/ai-governance routes for model cards, bias reports, approvals, SAMD assessments; swap pages; delete sample-ai-governance.ts.
Build migration-jobs route + table; swap /admin/migration pages; delete sample-migration.ts.
Build edge-fleet routes + sync-run tracking; swap /admin/edge/* pages; delete sample-edge-fleet.ts.
Build sensitive-data access routes (break-glass log, legal holds, proxy grants — last partially overlaps with caregiver-federation-routes); swap pages; delete sample-sensitive-data.ts.
Build CQM measure routes; swap /admin/cqm pages; delete sample-cqm.ts.
Build IG-profile routes; swap pages; delete sample-ig-profiles.ts.
Build security-posture routes; swap pages; delete sample-posture.ts.
Build extension/plugin routes; swap pages; delete sample-extensions.ts.

stages.name: Phase 3.7 — Work-app cleanup (deferrable)
stages.rationale: 5 sample files (dashboards, desk, resources, tasks, worklists) are only consumed by a separate work-app surface, NOT the admin app. Delete-only when their UIs are rewritten or retired.

baseline.summary: Sample-backed; work-app only.

current.summary: Done. 5 work-app sample files deleted (dashboards, desk, resources, tasks, worklists). Work-app pages /work/tasks/[id] + /forms/[taskId] swapped to fetchResource<Task> via generic FHIR CRUD. ZERO sample-*.ts files remain in apps/admin/src/app/_data/. ZERO orphan imports across apps/admin/src/. ADDITIONALLY: all 6 (office-admin) desk workspace tabs fully wired to real backends. Dashboard: 4 stat tiles from live /fhir/Appointment + /fhir/Patient?_summary=count + voiceAgentApi.getOverview('day'). Schedule: status-filter table from /fhir/Appointment with duration compute. Calls: paginated voiceAgentApi.listCalls() table with day/week/month window toggle. Patients: searchable /fhir/Patient?name= table with pagination. No-shows: /fhir/Appointment?status=noshow with last-7/30/90-day range filter. Settings: reminder policy + escalation threshold + no-show fee + urgent-line form (in-memory until desk-settings API ships). Typecheck clean post-rewrite.

history.note: Track created from user mandate: "I do not want any mock data. Everything should be wired in properly through backend services." Acknowledged as multi-week scope.

Brought up full local stack against real Postgres. Postgres 16 :5433 + MinIO + Redis + Mailpit + NATS healthy; 40 migrations applied; tenant-acme seeded (20 FHIR resources); apps/api :8080 returning real RSA jwks; admin :3000, trust :3001, patient :3002, installer :3003 all HTTP 200. Fixed 5 blockers: native PG port collision (override file → 5433), api Dockerfile missing scripts/ copy, migrate.js src/migrations resolver, duplicate POST /Connection registration (@cernia/server already provides FHIR CRUD), 122 partially-extracted pnpm packages (fix-pnpm-store.mjs).

Per-domain audit produced at docs/status/sample-data-elimination-plan.md. 30 sample files cataloged with status (0 wired / 11 partial / 12 needs-route / 7 deferrable), admin-page consumers, matching apps/api routes, effort estimate (~50 work-days), and 7-phase execution order.

3 sample files deleted (voice-budget, voice-improvements, questionnaires). 4 admin pages swapped to real backend (voice-budget incl. settings sub-page, voice-agent improvements, forms/[taskId]). New typed clients in /billing/voice-budget/api.ts + /voice-agent/improvements/api.ts. Pages return HTTP 200; typecheck + lint clean.

3 sample files deleted (tenants, iam, billing). 27 admin pages swapped + 5 backend CRUD routes added to admin-tenants-routes (GET list, GET :id, PATCH, DELETE soft-delete, GET transitions). IAM swap leveraged existing FHIR generic CRUD for User/Role/RoleBinding/ServiceAccount/ApiKey — no new endpoints needed. Billing swap leveraged existing /Subscription, /Invoice, /UsageEvent generic CRUD plus 3 custom operations. Also caught + fixed a pre-existing Next-14-vs-15 use(params) bug across 7 [id]/* pages that had been 500-ing on baseline. All 32 swapped pages HTTP 200; typecheck + lint clean.

3 sample files deleted (audit, reports, communications). 9 admin pages swapped. AuditEvent + Report use FHIR generic CRUD; communications moved to a static lib/communications.ts (no server endpoint; legitimate inline UI copy per the brief). Added AuditEvent types to @cernia/core public barrel. All 9 pages HTTP 200; typecheck + lint clean.

4 sample files deleted (cds, integrations, patient-portal, voice-agent). 17 admin pages swapped. CDS leverages /cds-services + generic /CdsService FHIR CRUD. Integrations uses /Connection generic CRUD with client-side protocol filter. Patient-portal uses /admin/patient-portal/* routes + generic /Patient CRUD. Voice-agent /lib/voice-agent-api.ts rewritten to drop mock-mode toggle. All 17 pages HTTP 200; typecheck + lint clean. TODOs: CDS invocations + integration transaction logs + portal link-issue actions still need apps/api endpoints (empty-state cards in UI).

3 sample files deleted (inference, marketplace, hie). 17 admin pages swapped. ML registry pages use /admin/ml/models routes; marketplace uses /marketplace/listings; HIE uses new typed wrappers in hie-api.ts. Pages without backend endpoints (jobs, batch, drift, evaluations, quotas, installs, reviews, payouts, hie/participation, hie/queries, hie/rls) render explicit empty-state cards — no fabricated data. 941/941 tests pass; typecheck + lint clean.

9 specialized sample files deleted (ai-governance, compliance, cqm, edge-fleet, extensions, ig-profiles, migration, posture, sensitive-data). 40+ admin pages now render empty-state cards explicitly stating 'Backend endpoint not yet shipped' — no fabricated data. Helper type files retained where domain shapes are needed for the empty-state DTO. Honest gap: per the original brief, these need ~25 work-days of backend endpoint + migration work to truly populate. Status=partial reflects 'sample data eliminated' but 'real data not yet present'.

5 work-app sample files deleted (dashboards, desk, resources, tasks, worklists). The audit was slightly wrong: 'desk' had 6 (office-admin) admin consumers, not work-app — those were converted to empty-state. Work-app pages /work/{tasks/[id], forms/[taskId]} swapped to fetchResource<Task> using generic FHIR CRUD. Final count: ZERO sample-*.ts files in apps/admin/src/app/_data/. ZERO orphan imports across apps/admin/src/. Dev server restarted once after Tailwind/webpack cache held stale references.

Desk workspace (all 7 (office-admin) tabs) fully wired to real backends. Dashboard: 4 stat tiles — live /fhir/Appointment date-range + /fhir/Patient?_summary=count + voiceAgentApi.getOverview('day'). Schedule: status-filter pills + appointment table with computed duration from /fhir/Appointment. Calls: paginated call log from voiceAgentApi.listCalls() with day/week/month window filter. Patients: searchable + paginated /fhir/Patient roster. No-shows: /fhir/Appointment?status=noshow with last-7/30/90-day range toggle. Settings: reminder/escalation/no-show-fee/urgent-line form (in-memory until desk-settings API endpoint ships). Waitlist: wired to new GET /waitlist/entries (resolves follow-up I-E-N5-admin-list-endpoint — added listEntriesForTenant to @cernia/scheduling PostgresWaitlistStore + route in apps/api waitlist-routes.ts). All 3 packages typecheck clean.

render superseded agents as green ✓ instead of red ⚠

agents.name: cost safeguards
agents.supersededNote: Full port complete via v3.5-engagement-full-integration: net-new files (voice-budget tracker, anomaly-detector, postgres-store, cost-ceilings, caller-rate-limiter, admin UI, voice-budget-routes, sql migration) landed via v3.4, and the voice-agent dialog hooks (CostCeilingTracker init, recordTurn, pre/post-LLM trip checks, bilingual ceiling utterance, transfer-to-human reason, costCeilingAudit emitter) landed via v3.5. 14 cost-ceiling integration tests pass.

agents.name: insurance eligibility
agents.supersededNote: Full port complete via v3.5-engagement-full-integration: net-new files (eligibility-service, eligibility-cache, eligibility-routes, X12 clearinghouses, insurance UIs) landed via v3.4, and the voice-agent verify-insurance intent + verify_insurance tool definition + encode270Request encoder + stub clearinghouse tests landed via v3.5.
